map:
PROVIDERS LAYER (Layer 0) — C:/Users/persi/Desktop/MCPenny/makecrazypenny/providers/. Files: __init__.py (package init + singleton), base.py (Provider ABC + auto-registration), registry.py (ProviderRegistry facade), cache.py (TTLCache L1/L2 + single-flight), ratelimit.py (TokenBucket), circuit.py (CircuitBreaker), plus concrete adapters: binance.py, bybit.py, coingecko.py, fear_greed.py (crypto, all keyless) and yfinance_provider/alpha_vantage/finnhub/fmp/edgar/stockwatcher/marketaux (stocks). Config lives in C:/Users/persi/Desktop/MCPenny/makecrazypenny/core/config.py.

(1) REGISTRATION PATTERN — two stages. Stage A (class discovery): base.py has module-level `PROVIDER_REGISTRY: list[type[Provider]]` and decorator `register_provider(cls)` (idempotent append). providers/__init__.py holds tuple `_PROVIDER_MODULES = ("yfinance_provider","alpha_vantage","finnhub","fmp","edgar","stockwatcher","marketaux","binance","bybit","coingecko","fear_greed")` and importlib-imports each inside try/except purely for the decorator side effect; a missing/broken module is silently skipped. Stage B (instantiation): `ProviderRegistry.default()` (registry.py) builds `Settings.from_env()`, instantiates every class in PROVIDER_REGISTRY (construction failure = skip), and `register(provider)` stores it in `_providers[provider.name]`, creates one `TokenBucket(provider.rate_per_min)` per `rate_key` (providers sharing an upstream key can share a bucket — rate_key defaults to name) and one `CircuitBreaker(fail_threshold=settings.circuit_fail_threshold [default 5, env MCP_CIRCUIT_FAIL_THRESHOLD], cooldown_s=settings.circuit_cooldown_s [60.0, MCP_CIRCUIT_COOLDOWN_S])` per provider name, plus one shared `TTLCache(settings.resolve_cache_dir(), l2_enabled=settings.l2_cache_enabled [MCP_L2_CACHE!=0])`. Process singleton via `get_registry()` / `reset_registry()` in __init__.py. CAPABILITY→PROVIDER mapping is NOT declared by providers: it is the static dict `CAPABILITY_CHAINS` in core/config.py (lines 53-75), capability name → ordered provider-name list = fallback order. Crypto chains: crypto_ohlcv [binance,bybit]; crypto_quote [binance,bybit,coingecko]; funding_rate/open_interest/long_short_ratio [binance,bybit]; crypto_sentiment [fear_greed]; crypto_global [coingecko]. Stock examples: sec_filings [edgar]; insider_transactions [finnhub,edgar]. Env overrides: MCP_CAPABILITY_CHAINS (JSON object) or MCP_CHAIN_<CAPABILITY> (comma list); unknown capability names in overrides are ignored. Capability names are FROZEN in `CAPABILITIES` tuple (config.py lines 28-51): 12 stock + 7 crypto (crypto_ohlcv, crypto_quote, funding_rate, open_interest, long_short_ratio, crypto_sentiment, crypto_global).

FETCH FLOW — `async ProviderRegistry.fetch(capability: str, *, ttl: float | None = None, **params) -> dict`. Walks the chain: skip if provider not registered / capability not in provider.supported / circuit.allow() False (reasons recorded per provider); then `await bucket.acquire(provider.cost)` (NO max_wait — waits indefinitely); then `await self._cache.get_or_fetch(key=(name, capability, params), ttl=effective_ttl, factory=lambda: provider.fetch(capability, **params))`. `MissingApiKey` and `NotImplementedError` = silent skip, do NOT trip breaker; any other exception → `circuit.record_failure()`, reason = `redact_secrets(f"{type}: {exc}")`, continue down chain. Success → `circuit.record_success()`, return envelope `{"provider": <name>, "data": <result.value>, "cached": <bool>}`. Chain exhausted → raise `AllProvidersFailed(capability, reasons)` (core/errors.py). TTL default per capability via `default_ttl(capability)` from `_DEFAULT_TTLS` (config.py lines 78-99): crypto_quote 15s, crypto_ohlcv 60s, funding_rate 60s, open_interest 60s, long_short_ratio 300s, crypto_sentiment 600s, crypto_global 300s; stocks: quote 15s, ohlcv 300s, news 600s, fundamentals/filings/insider 3600s; unknown → _FALLBACK_TTL 600s.

PRIMITIVES — cache.py: `TTLCache.get_or_fetch(key, ttl, factory) -> CacheResult(value, cached)`; L1 in-memory dict {key:(expires_at,value)}, L2 on-disk JSON `sha256(key).json` under cache dir (default %TEMP%/.mcpenny_cache, atomic os.replace, best-effort — disk errors degrade to L1-only); key = `make_key(provider, capability, params)` sorted-JSON (so cache is per-provider, not per-capability); async single-flight via per-key futures under an asyncio.Lock — followers get cached=True; leader's exception propagates and is pre-retrieved to silence asyncio warnings. ratelimit.py: `TokenBucket(rate_per_min, capacity=None)` continuous refill at rate/60 per sec, capacity defaults to rate_per_min (1-min burst), `acquire(cost=1, *, max_wait=None)` sleeps until tokens available, raises RateLimited only if max_wait set (registry never sets it); rate_per_min<=0 = unlimited. circuit.py: 3-state closed/open/half-open; `allow()` transitions open→half-open after cooldown_s permitting exactly one trial (concurrent callers blocked while half-open); record_success resets+closes; failure during half-open re-opens immediately; otherwise consecutive failures >= fail_threshold open it.

(2) ADDING A NEW KEYLESS PROVIDER (reddit / RSS / hyperliquid-info) — exact steps: (a) If a new capability is needed: in core/config.py add the name to the FROZEN `CAPABILITIES` tuple, add a `CAPABILITY_CHAINS` entry (ordered fallback list), add a `_DEFAULT_TTLS` entry (else 600s fallback applies); if a new normalized type is needed, add a frozen dataclass with `.to_dict()` and a `Provenance` field to core/types.py. (b) Create makecrazypenny/providers/<name>.py with this boilerplate: module docstring; `from __future__ import annotations`; only stdlib + `..core.types` + `.base` imports at top; `if TYPE_CHECKING: from ..core.config import Settings`; `@register_provider class XProvider(Provider):` with class attrs `name="x"`, `supported={"capability_a", ...}` (set), `rate_per_min=<N>` (sizes the shared bucket; 0=unlimited; precedents: binance 1200, bybit 600, edgar 300, fear_greed 60, coingecko 10), `cost=1`, `requires_key=None` (keyless); `__init__(self, settings)` calls `super().__init__(settings)` and may set `self.rate_key` (defaults to name); a private `async _get(path, params)` that does `import httpx  # lazy import (CONTRACT.md §2.2)` INSIDE the method, `async with httpx.AsyncClient(base_url=..., timeout=20.0) as client`, drops None params, `raise_for_status()`, returns `.json()`; a `_provenance()` helper returning `Provenance(provider=self.name, fetched_at=utcnow_iso(), cached=False)`; `async fetch(self, capability, **params)` that first calls `self.ensure_supported(capability)` (raises NotImplementedError → silent registry skip) then dispatches to per-capability handlers; handlers parse DEFENSIVELY (isinstance checks, module-local `_to_float`/`_ms_to_iso` coercers), RAISE (e.g. ValueError) when upstream returns no usable data so the registry falls through — never fabricate neutral values; return the core dataclass's `.to_dict()` (JSON-serializable dict or list of dicts). (c) Add the module name string to `_PROVIDER_MODULES` in providers/__init__.py. (d) Nothing else: bucket, circuit, cache, fallback, envelope, and redaction are all supplied by the registry automatically. (e) For configurable base URLs follow the `binance_base_url` pattern: a Settings field + `MCP_<X>_BASE_URL` env read in `Settings.from_env()`. (f) Tests: offline; either monkeypatch the provider's `_get` or inject a fake via `registry.register(fake)`; `reset_registry()` after env mutation. For a keyed provider instead set `requires_key="ENV_VAR_NAME"` and call `self.api_key()` (raises MissingApiKey → silent skip, breaker untouched). Envelope the orchestration layer sees: `{"provider", "data", "cached"}` where data embeds `"provenance": {"provider","fetched_at","cached"}`.

(3) BINANCE DERIVATIVES — binance.py, MAINNET `https://fapi.binance.com` (Settings.binance_base_url, overridable via MCP_BINANCE_BASE_URL for proxies/mirrors; NOT testnet — testnet exists only in the execution layer). Unauthenticated GETs via per-request httpx.AsyncClient (timeout 20s). Endpoints: crypto_ohlcv → `/fapi/v1/klines` (interval alias map, limit clamped 1..1500) → OHLCV; crypto_quote → `/fapi/v1/ticker/24hr` → Quote; funding_rate → `/fapi/v1/premiumIndex` (raises ValueError if lastFundingRate missing) + `/fapi/v1/fundingInfo` for per-symbol funding-interval hours (memoized 1h, default 8h, failure-tolerant) → FundingRate; open_interest → `/futures/data/openInterestHist` (period snapped to closest of 5m..1d via `_closest_period`, latest point = current OI, returns `{**OpenInterest.to_dict(), "history": [...]}`) with fallback `/fapi/v1/openInterest`; long_short_ratio → `/futures/data/globalLongShortAccountRatio` → LongShortRatio. Symbol normalized via `to_binance_perp` (core/symbols.py). rate_per_min=1200. Geo note: global Binance API returns HTTP 451 from US IPs — treated as a normal failure so the chain falls to bybit.py (api.bybit.com v5: /v5/market/kline newest-first re-sorted ascending, /v5/market/tickers gives price+funding+OI in one call, /v5/market/open-interest, /v5/market/account-ratio, /v5/market/instruments-info for fundingInterval minutes; rejects non-zero retCode; rate 600/min).

(4) HYPERLIQUID INFO API — NO registered Provider serves market data from Hyperliquid; no "hyperliquid" name appears in PROVIDER_REGISTRY or any CAPABILITY_CHAINS. Two non-provider paths DO hit it: (a) C:/Users/persi/Desktop/MCPenny/makecrazypenny/core/crypto_universe.py `_fetch_live_hyperliquid(base_url)` POSTs `{"type": "meta"}` to `{settings.hyperliquid_testnet_url}/info` (default https://api.hyperliquid-testnet.xyz, keyless, plain httpx, no SDK) to list tradable perps (name/szDecimals/maxLeverage, delisted filtered), cached 24h in `hyperliquid_perps.json` under the cache dir, exposed via `async fetch_hyperliquid_perps(*, settings=None, force_refresh=False)` (live→cache→"unavailable", never raises, no curated fallback) — used by the crypto screen and `paper_pairs` to constrain suggestions to HL-listed coins; it bypasses TTLCache/TokenBucket/CircuitBreaker entirely (own file cache, blocking httpx in asyncio.to_thread). (b) execution/hyperliquid.py uses the SDK `Info` client (testnet, skip_ws=True) for meta(), all_mids() (mid price), user_state(), spot_user_state(), frontend_open_orders()/open_orders() — execution-adjacent reads only. So funding/OI/price signals are sourced from Binance/Bybit mainnet while trades execute on HL testnet; no HL candles/funding/OI provider exists.

(5) MANDATORY CONVENTIONS for a new provider: import-safe — stdlib-only module top, `import httpx` lazily inside fetch/_get (commented "lazy import (CONTRACT.md §2.2)"), Settings imported under TYPE_CHECKING only; never network/keys at import; constructible without any env so ProviderRegistry.default() doesn't skip it. Keyless-first: `requires_key=None`; optional keys sent as headers when present (coingecko: `x-cg-demo-api-key`) but never required. SEC EDGAR specifically mandates a descriptive User-Agent on every request — `settings.edgar_user_agent`, default `"MakeCrazyPenny/0.1 (persico.mlo@gmail.com)"` (MCP_EDGAR_USER_AGENT), plus Accept/Accept-Encoding headers (edgar.py `_headers()`). Error contract: NotImplementedError for unsupported capability (via ensure_supported), MissingApiKey for absent required key (both = silent skip, no breaker trip); ValueError/any other exception for bad upstream data (= breaker-counted failure + fall-through); error text is redacted by the registry (core/redact.redact_secrets) before reaching reasons/UI. Return shape: core dataclass `.to_dict()` with embedded Provenance(provider, fetched_at=utcnow_iso(), cached=False); JSON-serializable (it gets written to L2 as JSON with default=str). Defensive parsing with per-module `_to_float`/`_ms_to_iso`. ASCII/DISCLAIMER are enforced at the CLI/server layer, not in providers (binance.py uses non-ASCII only inside docstrings); providers return data, the orchestration layer attaches DISCLAIMER. Tests offline/no-network (pytest, asyncio_mode=auto), fakes injected via registry.register or monkeypatched _get.

extension_points.how: New Provider subclass with @register_provider; add module name string to the tuple so the package import triggers registration
extension_points.notes: Broken/missing module is silently skipped, so a half-finished provider cannot break the package

extension_points.how: Add new capability name + ordered fallback chain + cache TTL; chains overridable at runtime via MCP_CAPABILITY_CHAINS (JSON) or MCP_CHAIN_<CAPABILITY> (csv), but ONLY for capabilities already in the base dict
extension_points.notes: Capability vocabulary is FROZEN by convention; a new capability is a config.py edit, not env-only

extension_points.how: Add a frozen dataclass with .to_dict() and a Provenance field for any new normalized payload (pattern: FundingRate, OpenInterest, SentimentScore)
extension_points.notes: Providers must return to_dict() output, JSON-serializable for the L2 cache

extension_points.how: Runtime injection point — tests and swarm code can register fakes or extra instances by name without touching auto-discovery
extension_points.notes: Also creates the TokenBucket (per rate_key, shareable) and CircuitBreaker (per name)

extension_points.how: Add base-URL/env knobs following the binance_base_url + MCP_BINANCE_BASE_URL pattern; rebuild singleton with reset_registry() after env changes

Pattern for non-capability cached fetchers (live -> file cache -> fallback, asyncio.to_thread, never raises) — the right template for a hyperliquid-info market-data path or any universe/listing feed that doesn't fit the capability vocabulary
extension_points.notes: Already POSTs {type: 'meta'} to the HL testnet /info endpoint keylessly; extending it (or promoting it to a real Provider) is the natural place for HL allMids/candles/funding

extension_points.how: Per-call TTL override for callers needing fresher-than-default data (e.g. a fast loop passing ttl=5)

conventions: Import-safe: stdlib-only at module top; `import httpx` lazily inside fetch/_get with comment 'lazy import (CONTRACT.md §2.2)'; Settings imported under TYPE_CHECKING only; no network or key reads at import; class must construct without any env vars

Register via @register_provider and list the module in _PROVIDER_MODULES; declare class attrs name (unique), supported (set of FROZEN capability names), rate_per_min (0 = unlimited; binance 1200, bybit 600, edgar 300, fear_greed 60, coingecko 10), cost=1, requires_key (None for keyless); rate_key defaults to name, share it across providers that share an upstream quota

fetch() contract: call self.ensure_supported(capability) first (NotImplementedError = silent registry skip); raise MissingApiKey via self.api_key() for keyed providers (silent skip, no breaker trip); raise ValueError/anything else for missing/bad upstream data so the chain falls through — never return fabricated neutral values (see binance/bybit funding_rate)

Return shape: normalized core dataclass .to_dict() (or list of them), JSON-serializable, embedding Provenance(provider=self.name, fetched_at=utcnow_iso(), cached=False); the registry wraps it as {'provider', 'data', 'cached'} and raises AllProvidersFailed(capability, reasons) on exhaustion
Defensive parsing: module-local _to_float/_ms_to_iso best-effort coercers; isinstance-check every payload level; clamp limits (e.g. max(1, min(int(limit), 1500)))

HTTP: per-request httpx.AsyncClient with explicit timeout (~20s), drop None params, raise_for_status(); provider-specific protocol checks (bybit rejects non-zero retCode); EDGAR REQUIRES descriptive User-Agent from settings.edgar_user_agent plus Accept headers; CoinGecko optional x-cg-demo-api-key header only when key present

Resilience is supplied by the registry, never reimplemented in providers: shared TTLCache (L1 mem + L2 sha256-named JSON files, single-flight), TokenBucket per rate_key, CircuitBreaker per provider (5 failures / 60s cooldown defaults), redact_secrets on failure reasons

Cache TTLs are per-capability in _DEFAULT_TTLS (crypto_quote 15s, crypto_ohlcv/funding_rate/open_interest 60s, long_short_ratio/crypto_global 300s, crypto_sentiment 600s; unknown 600s); per-call override via registry.fetch(ttl=...)
ASCII output and DISCLAIMER are enforced at the CLI/MCP layer, not inside providers; provider data carries provenance instead
Tests offline/no-network: fakes via registry.register or monkeypatched _get; reset_registry() after env mutation; pytest asyncio_mode=auto
gaps: No Hyperliquid market-data provider: signals (funding, OI, long/short, candles) come from Binance/Bybit MAINNET while execution is HL TESTNET — venue divergence in funding rates, prices, and listings; the keyless HL /info POST pattern already exists in core/crypto_universe.py but only fetches the perp meta listing, and it bypasses the registry's cache/ratelimit/circuit machinery entirely
REST polling only — no websocket/streaming anywhere; freshest data is bounded by TTLs (15s quotes, 60s funding/OI), too coarse for an always-on swarm reacting intra-minute

All resilience state is per-process and asyncio-local: TokenBucket, CircuitBreaker, L1 cache, and single-flight futures are not shared across processes — N swarm agents each get the FULL rate budget (N x 1200/min against Binance) and independent circuits; only the L2 file cache is shared (atomic writes, but no cross-process single-flight, so concurrent misses all hit upstream)

registry.fetch calls bucket.acquire(cost) with NO max_wait — a drained bucket blocks that fetch indefinitely instead of failing over to the next provider; RateLimited surfacing exists in TokenBucket but is never used by the registry

No retry/backoff/jitter within a provider attempt — one transient HTTP blip counts as a circuit failure and falls through the chain; no negative caching, so a flapping symbol re-hits upstream every call until the breaker opens
New httpx.AsyncClient per request — no connection pooling/keep-alive; meaningful overhead at swarm call volumes

Cache keys include the provider name, so a Binance-geo-blocked process re-pays the full Bybit fetch even when an identical Binance-keyed result concept exists; no capability-level cache sharing across the fallback chain

Single-provider chains are SPOFs: crypto_sentiment=[fear_greed] only, crypto_global=[coingecko] only (10/min keyless); no crypto news/social/RSS/reddit provider exists at all — per-coin sentiment is absent
Adding a capability requires editing core/config.py (FROZEN CAPABILITIES tuple + chains + TTLs); env overrides only reorder chains for capabilities already known

No observability: no metrics/counters for cache hit rate, bucket waits, circuit state transitions, or per-provider latency — a swarm cannot detect a silently degraded chain except via AllProvidersFailed; circuit/bucket state is also not introspectable through any MCP tool
L2 cache is never pruned (sha256-named JSON files accumulate forever under %TEMP%/.mcpenny_cache); fine for a CLI, unbounded for an always-on process

TokenBucket starts full on every process start (full burst on restart) and CircuitBreaker state resets — a crash-looping agent can hammer upstreams; half-open admits exactly one trial and blocks all others with no trial timeout, so a hung trial call wedges the provider
fetch envelope carries only a boolean 'cached' — no age/fetched_at at the envelope level (it is buried in data.provenance), making staleness-aware decisions awkward for downstream agents
